questions.q: In which year was the Rowlatt Satyagraha launched?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Satyagraha was launched in 1919 against the newly passed Rowlatt Act.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Satyagraha was a protest against which piece of legislation?
questions.explanation: The satyagraha was a protest against the Rowlatt Act, which permitted detention without trial.

questions.q: Who called the nationwide Rowlatt Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi called the satyagraha against the Rowlatt Act in 1919.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act gave the colonial government wide powers to do what?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act allowed the government to detain suspects without trial, sparking the protest.

questions.q: The subtitle 'No Appeal, No Plea, No Lawyer' refers to the harsh nature of which law?
questions.explanation: The slogan 'No Appeal, No Plea, No Lawyer' captured the denial of legal rights under the Rowlatt Act.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Satyagraha is notable as the first all-India agitation led by whom?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Satyagraha marked the first nationwide political agitation conducted under Gandhi's leadership.

questions.q: What form of resistance did the Rowlatt Satyagraha primarily take?
questions.explanation: The satyagraha was a non-violent protest, in keeping with Gandhi's method of resistance.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act was widely opposed because it suspended ordinary legal protections during what kind of period?
questions.explanation: Indians were angered that wartime-style emergency powers were being extended into peacetime through the Rowlatt Act.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Satyagraha called on people to observe which form of nationwide protest?
questions.explanation: Gandhi called for a hartal, a general suspension of work and business, as part of the Rowlatt Satyagraha.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Satyagraha took place against the backdrop of unrest in which region of India in particular?
questions.explanation: Punjab saw especially intense protest and repression during the Rowlatt agitation, culminating in the Amritsar tragedy.

questions.q: After whom was the Rowlatt Act named?
questions.explanation: The Act was named after Sir Sidney Rowlatt, the British judge who chaired the committee that recommended it.

questions.q: On what date did Gandhi first call for a nationwide hartal as part of the Rowlatt Satyagraha?
questions.explanation: Gandhi fixed 6 April 1919 for the nationwide hartal against the Rowlatt Act, after an initial earlier date.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act is sometimes called the 'Black Act' because of its denial of which rights?
options: Rights to a fair trial and appeal
questions.explanation: Critics called the Rowlatt Act the 'Black Act' for stripping accused persons of the right to trial and appeal.

questions.q: To organize the Rowlatt Satyagraha, Gandhi helped form which body?
questions.explanation: Gandhi founded the Satyagraha Sabha to coordinate the campaign against the Rowlatt Act.

questions.q: The Rowlatt bills were based on the recommendations of a committee investigating what?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Committee was set up to investigate revolutionary conspiracies, and its recommendations led to the Act.

questions.q: How did most elected Indian members of the Imperial Legislative Council respond to the Rowlatt Bill?
questions.explanation: Indian members of the council opposed the Rowlatt Bill, and some resigned, but it was passed over their objections.

questions.q: Which prominent leader resigned his membership of the Imperial Legislative Council in protest at the Rowlatt Act?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah resigned from the Imperial Legislative Council in protest against the Rowlatt Act.

questions.q: The repression during the Rowlatt agitation in Punjab led most directly to which tragic event?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt unrest in Punjab culminated in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre at Amritsar on 13 April 1919.

questions.q: What did the Rowlatt Satyagraha urge Indians to do toward the unjust law?
options: Petition the King in writing only
questions.explanation: The satyagraha called on Indians to peacefully defy the Rowlatt Act through civil disobedience.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act formally extended emergency powers that had been introduced during which conflict?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act sought to continue wartime emergency detention powers from the First World War into peacetime.

questions.q: The confusion over dates of the Rowlatt hartal arose because Gandhi had initially announced which earlier date?
questions.explanation: Gandhi first announced 30 March 1919, then changed it to 6 April, causing confusion as some observed both.

questions.q: Two popular Punjabi leaders whose arrest inflamed the Rowlatt protests in Amritsar were Dr Satyapal and whom?
questions.explanation: The arrest and deportation of Dr Satyapal and Dr Saifuddin Kitchlew enraged the people of Amritsar during the Rowlatt agitation.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act's official short title referred to it as an act concerning which kind of offences?
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act was officially the Anarchical and Revolutionary Crimes Act of 1919.

questions.q: As part of the Rowlatt Satyagraha, Gandhi proposed the civil disobedience of selling which prohibited publications?
options: Banned books such as Hind Swaraj
questions.explanation: Gandhi planned the symbolic civil disobedience of selling proscribed literature, including his own banned writings such as Hind Swaraj.

questions.q: Gandhi later admitted the Rowlatt Satyagraha had been a 'Himalayan miscalculation' chiefly because of what?
options: People were not ready to remain non-violent
questions.explanation: Gandhi called it a 'Himalayan miscalculation' on realizing the masses were not yet disciplined enough to stay non-violent.

questions.q: During the Rowlatt agitation Gandhi was stopped and turned back by authorities while trying to enter which province?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was detained and ordered back as he tried to travel into Punjab during the Rowlatt protests, fuelling further anger.

questions.q: Of the two Rowlatt bills introduced, how many were actually passed into law in 1919?
questions.explanation: Of the two bills proposed, only one was enacted as the Rowlatt Act in March 1919.

questions.q: The pledge taken by satyagrahis against the Rowlatt Act committed them to disobey the law while also doing what?
options: Refusing to pay any taxes ever
questions.explanation: The Satyagraha pledge bound members to civilly disobey the Rowlatt laws while faithfully refraining from violence.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Act was eventually repealed; this came as part of the colonial government's review in which early-1920s context?
options: Amid mounting agitation, it was repealed in 1922
questions.explanation: Facing sustained criticism, the government repealed the Rowlatt Act in 1922 without ever having used it widely.

questions.q: The Rowlatt Satyagraha is historically significant as the moment Gandhi emerged as the leader of what?
options: A truly nationwide mass political movement
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Satyagraha marked Gandhi's emergence at the head of an all-India mass political movement for the first time.
